name: npcap
description: Install Npcap packet capture driver (WinPcap-compat, loopback, non-admin). Matches mastw, which also runs npcapwatchdog scheduled task to keep the npcap service alive. Wireshark and any other capture-based tooling depend on this.
verify:
powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "${log}=Join-Path (Join-Path $env:SystemDrive 'MAST') 'logs\verify
pcap-verify.log'; ${smoke}=Join-Path (Join-Path $env:SystemDrive 'MAST') 'logs\smoke
pcap-smoke.txt'; ${svc}=Get-Service -Name 'npcap' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; ${drv}='C:\Windows\System32\Npcap
pcap.sys'; if (${svc} -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath ${drv})) { Set-Content -Path ${log} -Value ('npcap Status=' + ${svc}.Status + ' StartType=' + ${svc}.StartType + ' driver=' + ${drv}); Set-Content -Path ${smoke} -Value 'npcap_ok' } else { ('npcap service or driver missing (svc=' + ${svc} + ' drvExists=' + (Test-Path -LiteralPath ${drv}) + ')') | Out-File -FilePath ${log} -Encoding UTF8; exit 1 }"
